spec_match.rationale: The comparison now uses current native syntax for the new radiogroup, focusgroup="radiogroup block wrap", while the old side still demonstrates the roving-tabindex implementation.

interactive.rationale: Both columns are live, the old column supports keyboard/click selection, and new remove/reset controls demonstrate the active-item removal edge case.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now quantifies the old implementation cost and labels the new side as one native behavior attribute instead of an invalid vertical/wrap-only value.

edge_cases.rationale: The focus-loss recovery case is now demonstrated, but writing-mode or orientation auto-inference is still not included.

design_system.rationale: Variables drive the comparison panels, status readouts, controls, and badges; the page remains an interactive comparison rather than a static code card.

openQuestions.title: Add orientation auto-inference
openQuestions.detail: The old code hard-codes the vertical axis; the new attribute supports block and inline modifiers that adapt to writing-mode. Add a writing-mode: vertical-rl toggle so the comparison includes the internationalization win, not just line count and mutation recovery.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v146/focusgroup/roving-tabindex-compare/: the page loaded with no console/network errors, the new side used focusgroup="radiogroup block wrap", old removal reported focus loss to the document body, new removal promoted Oldest first, and both reset buttons restored four options. The previous line-count and focus-loss questions are resolved.
